name: Ísafjörður
summary: Ísafjörður is the biggest town in Iceland's wild Westfjords, a row of colourful timber houses tucked under towering cliffs on a curving sandy spit beside a deep blue fjord.
funFacts: Ísafjörður's old town is full of brightly painted timber houses, some over 250 years old, making them among the oldest buildings in all of Iceland.
The Arctic fox is Iceland's only native land mammal. It walked across the frozen sea from Greenland thousands of years ago, before any people arrived.
Dynjandi waterfall thunders down 100 metres in a wide, lacy curtain shape. Its name actually means 'thunderous' in Icelandic because of how loudly it roars.
Ísafjörður's airport is one of the trickiest in the world. Pilots must do a sharp turn between mountains seconds before landing, almost like a video game level.
On tiny Vigur island, just offshore, around 100,000 puffins nest each summer alongside thousands of eider ducks and noisy Arctic terns.
thingsToDo: Wander Ísafjörður's old town to spot the colourful timber houses, some more than 250 years old.
Explore the Westfjords Heritage Museum, packed with old fishing boats, diving suits, and historic accordions.
Hike across cliffs and empty valleys at Hornstrandir nature reserve, looking out for friendly Arctic foxes.
Drive to giant Dynjandi waterfall and climb the rocky path past six smaller falls to its thundering top.
Take a boat to Vigur island to walk among thousands of puffins, terns and soft-feathered eider ducks.
Ride up to the Bolafjall viewing platform to gaze straight down 600 metres at the wild ocean below.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Small black and white seabird with a bright orange beak that nests in clifftop burrows.

wildlife.name: Arctic Fox
wildlife.description: Iceland's only native land mammal, with thick fur that turns white in snowy winter.

wildlife.name: Common Eider
wildlife.description: Large sea duck with super soft down feathers used by people to stuff warm pillows.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Huge ocean giant that often swims into the calm Westfjords to feed in summer.

images.alt: A street of colourful old timber houses in Ísafjörður's historic centre.
Old black timber buildings of the Westfjords Heritage Museum beside Ísafjörður's harbour.
Towering green sea cliffs at Hornstrandir nature reserve north of Ísafjörður.
The huge Dynjandi waterfall tumbling down a mountain like a giant bridal veil.
Tiny green Vigur island in the fjord, home to thousands of puffins and eider ducks.
The wide view from the top of Bolafjall mountain looking out over Bolungarvík and the sea.
A puffin with its bright orange beak standing on grass on Vigur island.
A fluffy brown Arctic fox standing on Icelandic grass in its summer coat.
A male common eider duck with black and white feathers floating on calm water.
A humpback whale leaping high out of the ocean.
